narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically American) foreign policy and perception, portraying it as hysterical, exaggerated, and fundamentally dishonest in its portrayal of Russia as a threat.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text predicts and seemingly advocates for a catastrophic nuclear war as a consequence of Western political actions, framing it as an inevitable negative outcome caused by Western 'un-elected bureaucrats.'

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement explicitly evokes fear and anxiety about the ultimate consequence of the conflict: a nuclear war that wipes out humanity. It presents this as a plausible or even desired outcome given the current trajectory.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly questions the reliability and motives of Western media, accusing it of misrepresenting the war's progress ('framing the war as if Russia is losing') and causing harmful consequences, which aligns with the 'Distrust towards Media' narrative.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The title and premise of the discussion explicitly speculate on the outcome of the war ('Russia is Winning') and the potential consequences ('Nuclear Threat'), fitting the definition of predicting or making assumptions about conflict results.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly and harshly criticizes and dehumanizes the people of a Western nation (America), aligning with the narrative of discrediting the West through extreme negative portrayal.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: we deserve EVERYTHING we get
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement, in the context of the preceding text blaming un-elected bureaucrats, implies that Western populations will face severe negative consequences (like nuclear war) for the actions of their governments, highlighting adverse outcomes for the West.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This shifts blame for the continuation of the war and the resulting death away from Russia (the invader) and onto Western media and unnamed actors who are 'desperate' to keep it going.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion..
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically American) foreign policy and perception, accusing it of being hysterical and creating a false threat narrative against Russia and China, which aligns with the definition of discrediting Western diplomatic stances and actions.
